evals.name: flowchart-with-decision-branch
evals.prompt: make me a flowchart for the signup flow on listingview — user enters email + password, we send a verification email, they click the link, account activates. Also show what happens if the verification link expires (we let them request a new one).
evals.expected_output: Excalidraw scene JSON (Mode B) showing a vertical flowchart with a Start node, signup step, send-verification step, a decision diamond ('Link still valid?' or similar), branching to either 'Account activated' (success path) or 'Request new link' → loops back. Arrows must be bound via startBinding/endBinding, decision diamond should have yes/no branch labels as small text near the arrows. Happy-path arrows can use one accent color.

assertions.text: Output is valid Excalidraw scene JSON with type='excalidraw' wrapper
Includes at least one diamond element used for the expiry decision (not a rectangle)
Every arrow has both startBinding and endBinding set to real element IDs
Shape labels are bound via containerId, not floating standalone text
Layout flows top-to-bottom (later steps have higher y values than earlier ones)
No two nodes have overlapping bounding boxes

evals.name: saas-architecture-with-tiers
evals.prompt: draw the listingview architecture: nextjs frontend on vercel, node/express api on aws, postgres on aws, the chrome extension that injects on etsy.com, and the framer marketing site at listingview.io. show how they connect — like the extension talks to the api, the api talks to postgres, the marketing site is standalone but points to the app. group by tier if it helps.
evals.expected_output: Excalidraw scene JSON showing the 5 components, organized either by tier (frontend / API / data) using frame elements as containers, or by deployment provider. Arrows show meaningful connections only (not every-to-every). Each component is a labeled rectangle. The Chrome extension is visually distinct (e.g., dashed border or different stroke color) since it lives in a different environment. The marketing site is shown as a separate cluster.

assertions.text: Output is valid Excalidraw scene JSON with type='excalidraw' wrapper
Contains at least one frame element used to group components by tier or environment
All 5 named components appear as labeled shapes (Next.js frontend, Express API, Postgres, Chrome extension, Framer site)
Connecting arrows are bound to elements (not floating coordinates)
Not every component connects to every other component — connections are selective
Components within the same frame have matching frameId values

evals.name: slide-deck-presentation
evals.prompt: i'm putting together a quick pitch deck for a youtube sponsorship — 4 slides max: 1) intro (who i am + what listingview does), 2) audience (etsy sellers, ~X reach), 3) the ask (sponsorship slots, rate), 4) thanks/next steps. make it as an excalidraw deck so i can edit it.
evals.expected_output: Excalidraw scene JSON with exactly 4 frame elements arranged left-to-right at 854x480 each, ~350px gaps. Each frame contains a title (standalone text, larger font) and body content (text bullets or small rectangles). Every element inside a frame has frameId pointing to that frame. Slide titles match the 4 requested topics. No multi-line text crammed inside circles.

assertions.text: Output is valid Excalidraw scene JSON with type='excalidraw' wrapper
Contains exactly 4 frame elements
Every frame has width=854 and height=480
Frames are arranged left-to-right (each frame's x is greater than the previous)
Gap between consecutive frames is approximately 350px
Every non-frame element has a frameId pointing to one of the 4 frames
Slide titles are standalone text elements (not bound labels)
Frame roughness is 0 (clean slide edges, not hand-drawn)
